" LEA III charge.


A meeting of the newspaper men
has been called for Wednesday af-
ternoon at the assembly room of
the chamber of commerce to ar
range for the entertainment of the
visiting correspondents who will
come to report the Dry Farming
congress. tt


There is some feeling of resent
ment against the new rule of the
Y. M. c. A. prohibiting smoking in
the building. Six have resigned
and four others threaten to walk
out


The law school Of the Spokane
college will open tonight.


DR.. J. G. GundIach, a war vet
eran, is confined to his home with
stroke of paralysis.


Mrs. Frank SoIlars, held in the
county Jail for shooting at her hus-
band, who has sued her for a di-
vOrce, has been released on her
own recognizance, at the request of
Jail Matron Warn.


D. GoIdstein, who charges negli-
gence causing a gas explosion, has
sued the Spokane Falls Gas Co, for
$4,000 damages.


Mrs. Christian Dale was injured
in the wreck at Gibbs a year ago,
and recovered judgment against
the Inland for $500. Judge Hinkle
has now cut the verdict to $500,
which the plaintiff must accept or
take a new trial.


Ten vags were rounded up yes.
terday by the police.


Carl johnson, a fisherman, 80,
ELF First avenue, was drowned in
Clear Lake under peculiar circum-
stances yesterday morning.


Detectives are searching for n.
II. Dennison, Standpoint, Idaho, who
has been missing for ten days and
may have committed suicide.


While feeding threshinq ma.
chine near Rosalia yesterday, Mack
Shields, So, was caught in the belt
and seriously but not fatally in
jured.


Medals have arrived in Spokane
from Washington for those who
qualified in the militia marksmen
contests in 1909.


Charles jackson, colored, E10t7
First avenue, is locked up ON a
charge of beating his white wife
during row Saturday night.


Fred Aren't, SO, from Buffalo, ~.
Y, died at the emergency hospital
yesterday as the result of a sud
den hemorrhage.


Charles J. Johnson, "a, told the
police a spectacular story early
Sunday morning of how he had
killed his brother near Portland,
Ore, five years ago to get the lin
surance money. Since then he has
denied all recollection of his cDn-
fession and Chief Sullivan believes
the man may be laboring under a
mental delusion, suffering from a
rolioinnc mania
